description: AR300M / AR300M-Ext / AR300MD (NAND boot, switch LEFT)

switch.description: Physical slide switch. RECOMMENDED: always leave LEFT (toward USB) for NAND boot. NAND is 128MB and faster; NOR is only 16MB.
switch.recommendation: Always use NAND (switch LEFT). NOR serves as automatic failsafe — if NAND fails 3 times, U-Boot falls back to NOR automatically.
switch.purpose: Selects NAND vs NOR boot when boot_dev=on. U-Boot source: switch_boot_load() returns val=0 (switch RIGHT) → bootm 0x9f050000 (NOR), val=1/2 (switch LEFT) → nboot (NAND). GL_BOOT_ADDR=0x9f050000 is the NOR firmware address. Switch-based NOR boot may not work on all U-Boot versions — use bootcount method as fallback.

left.label: Toward USB/reset button
left.gpio_state: GPIO 0 or 1 HIGH (val=1 or 2 → nboot NAND)
left.notes: Boots kernel from NAND mtd4, rootfs from NAND UBI mtd5. This is the default and recommended boot source.

right.label: Away from USB port
right.gpio_state: GPIO 0,1 both LOW (val=0 → bootm GL_BOOT_ADDR = 0x9f050000 = NOR firmware address)
right.notes: Switch RIGHT triggers NOR boot via bootm 0x9f050000. GL_BOOT_ADDR is set from BOOT_ADDR in configs/ar300m-ar71xx_generic.mk = 0x9f050000 (NOR firmware partition). May not work reliably on all U-Boot versions — GL.iNet forum reports from 2017-2018 say switch NOR boot broken. Possibly caused by NAND-specific bootargs causing NOR kernel panic. Retest pending with correct bootargs.

uboot_env.boot_dev: Set 'on' via fw_setenv boot_dev on to enable switch-based boot selection. Enables GPIO reading via switch_boot_load(). Default: not set (router always boots NAND).
uboot_env.bootlimit: 3 failed NAND boots triggers automatic NOR fallback (altbootcmd=run blf).
uboot_env.bootcmd: if nand bad; then nboot 0x81000000 0 || run blf; else run blf; fi — always tries NAND first. select_boot_dev() runs BEFORE bootcmd in GL.iNet U-Boot and handles switch-based selection.
uboot_env.bootargs: Must be generic for dual-boot: 'console=ttyS0,115200 rootfstype=squashfs,jffs2 noinitrd'. Default contains 'root=/dev/mtdblock8 ubi.mtd=5' which is NAND-only and causes NOR kernel panic.

recovery-http.description: U-Boot HTTP recovery via pepe2k/u-boot_mod. Hold reset button while powering on. Always boots NAND regardless of switch position — switch only affects normal boot.
recovery-http.reset_instructions: Hold reset button while connecting power. Hold for ~5 seconds until LEDs flash. Switch position does not matter for U-Boot recovery.
recovery-http.led_pattern: LEDs flash 5 times during U-Boot entry
recovery-http.firmware_note: Use the factory.img (e.g. glinet_gl-ar300m-nand-squashfs-factory.img) for first-time NAND flash. NOT initramfs — the factory.img writes directly to NAND. For subsequent updates, use sysupgrade from running OpenWrt.

nor_recovery.description: Set up NOR flash as a recovery OpenWrt partition. Creates a dual-boot setup: NAND (switch LEFT) = primary, NOR = recovery. NOR boot can be triggered via switch RIGHT (with boot_dev=on, designed to call bootm 0x9f050000) or via bootcount method (fw_setenv bootcount 3). Switch-based NOR boot may not work on all U-Boot versions — use bootcount as reliable fallback.
nor_recovery.warning: Flashing U-Boot (mtd0) can brick the device if power is lost during write. Only recoverable via UART serial.

uboot_upgrade.reboot_reason: U-Boot is loaded at boot time; new version takes effect after reboot

nor_firmware.description: Stock OpenWrt for NOR recovery partition. Minimal, clean, always boots fresh — no custom config that could be corrupted.

boot_env.description: Enables switch-based boot selection: LEFT=NAND (nboot), RIGHT=NOR (bootm 0x9f050000). Switch-based NOR boot may not work on all U-Boot versions. Use bootcount method as reliable fallback.

boot_method.description: Set fw_setenv bootcount 3 and power cycle. U-Boot increments to 4, exceeds bootlimit=3, sets nand_boot_failed=1 and changes bootcmd to bootm 0x9f050000. CAVEAT: With working NAND kernel, select_boot_dev() runs nboot first which boots NAND and never returns — NOR bootm is never reached. Bootcount NOR boot may only work when NAND kernel is broken (nboot fails/returns, allowing fallthrough to NOR bootm).
boot_method.switch_method_status: Switch RIGHT (val=0) triggers bootm 0x9f050000 (NOR address) in select_boot_dev(). Tested 2026-05-17: NOR kernel starts but PANICS. ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED from source: mips_cache_flush() in cmd_bootm.c runs BEFORE LZMA decompression, not after. Decompressed kernel at 0x80060000 stays dirty in D-cache, I-cache fetches stale RAM. bootm 0xbf050000 (uncached alias) ALSO FAILS — destination writes still dirty D-cache. NAND also has the same bug but survives probabilistically (D-cache pre-state from DMA reads causes corruption to land in non-critical code). Fix: flush_cache after decompression (applied in fork https://github.com/Amperstrand/uboot-for-qca95xx branch conwrt-fix). CI green, artifact built. NOT YET TESTED ON DEVICE.

tested_2026-05-17.switch_method: FAILED — switch RIGHT, boot_dev=on. bootm 0x9f050000 (KSEG0 cached). NOR kernel starts but panics. Root cause: D-cache not flushed after LZMA decompression (cmd_bootm.c:325 vs 390).
tested_2026-05-17.bootcount_method: UNRELIABLE — With working NAND, nboot boots NAND and never returns to NOR bootm. Previously worked when NAND was broken. Same D-cache flush bug applies.
tested_2026-05-17.nor_partition_verified: MD5 of NOR mtd2 matches source image exactly. Valid uImage header (LZMA comp, MIPS OpenWrt Linux-6.6.93). Same kernel version as NAND (6.6.93, 14 bytes smaller).
tested_2026-05-17.uncached_test: TESTED bootm 0xbf050000 (KSEG1 uncached alias) via boot_dev=off + custom bootcmd. ALSO FAILED. Uncached source reads don't help — destination writes at 0x80060000 still dirty D-cache. Device required U-Boot safe mode recovery.
tested_2026-05-17.root_cause_confirmed: cmd_bootm.c lines 321-406: mips_cache_flush() runs BEFORE decompression. No flush between LZMA output and kernel jump. MIPS 24Kc Harvard arch: I-cache fills from RAM, can't see dirty D-cache. Fix: add flush_cache(hdr->ih_load, unc_len) after decompression.

sources.firmware_format: NAND image is UBI (tar/uImage+UBI), NOR image is raw squashfs (uImage). NOT interchangeable. Source: openwrt/openwrt target/linux/ath79/image/nand.mk — Device/glinet_gl-ar300m-nand uses 'append-ubi', Device/glinet_gl-ar300m-nor uses default NOR layout. Verified 2026-05-17: NAND sysupgrade=POSIX tar, NOR sysupgrade=u-boot uImage.
sources.uboot_source: https://github.com/gl-inet/uboot-for-qca95xx — GL.iNet fork of pepe2k/u-boot_mod. Binary: uboot-gl-ar300m-20220216. Patched fork with flush_cache fix: https://github.com/Amperstrand/uboot-for-qca95xx (branch conwrt-fix). CI-built u-boot.bin available as artifact from GitHub Actions run 25995448558.
sources.dual_boot_docs: https://docs.gl-inet.com/router/en/3/specification/gl-ar300m/ — 'The router has two flash so we have two firmwares.' Switch selects boot source when boot_dev=on.
sources.openwrt_wiki: https://openwrt.org/toh/gl.inet/gl-ar300m — 'The suggested way of installing OpenWrt on the GL-AR300M is using the u-boot bootloader.'
sources.risk_assessment: U-Boot flash (mtd0) is the only dangerous operation. Power loss during mtd0 write = brick, recoverable only via UART serial. NOR firmware flash (mtd2) is safe — NAND boot is unaffected. Failsafe: 3 failed NAND boots auto-fallback to NOR (bootlimit=3, altbootcmd=run blf).
sources.bootargs_fix: CRITICAL: Default U-Boot bootargs contains 'root=/dev/mtdblock8 ubi.mtd=5,2048' which is NAND-specific. NOR kernel uses different MTD layout (only ~5 partitions) so mtdblock8 doesn't exist. Must change bootargs to 'console=ttyS0,115200 rootfstype=squashfs,jffs2 noinitrd' for dual-boot to work. Source: gl-inet/uboot-for-qca95xx src/common/device_check.c and src/include/configs/board953x.h — bootargs hardcoded for NAND layout.
sources.boot_local_env: GL.iNet U-Boot has 'boot_local' env var as alternative to GPIO-based switch. boot_local=nor forces NOR boot, boot_local=nand forces NAND. Checked in select_boot_dev() after switch_boot_load(). Source: gl-inet/uboot-for-qca95xx src/common/device_check.c lines 95-105.
sources.gpio_switch: Slider switch reads hardware GPIO 0 and 1 (mask 0x3) via AR7240_GPIO_IN register. switch_boot_load() debounces by reading twice with 20ms delay. val=0 (both LOW, switch RIGHT) → bootm GL_BOOT_ADDR (0x9f050000 = NOR address). val=1 or 2 (switch LEFT) → nboot 0x81000000 0 (NAND). val=3 → fall through. GL_BOOT_ADDR is the NOR firmware partition address, NOT a NAND address. Source: gl-inet/uboot-for-qca95xx configs/ar300m-ar71xx_generic.mk:24, src/board/atheros/board953x/board953x.c:426-438, src/common/device_check.c:71-113.

post_setup_verification.nand_boot: Switch LEFT, power cycle → NAND OpenWrt (primary, hostname from NAND overlay). TESTED 2026-05-17: works correctly, bootcount auto-resets to 0.
post_setup_verification.nor_boot: Bootcount method: fw_setenv bootcount 3, power cycle → NOR OpenWrt (recovery, hostname ar300-nor-recovery). After NOR boot, reset bootcount to 0 via fw_setenv bootcount 0 to restore normal NAND boot.
post_setup_verification.nor_boot_switch_method: BROKEN — do not rely on switch position for NOR boot. Use bootcount method.
post_setup_verification.failsafe: 3 failed NAND boots auto-fallback to NOR (bootlimit=3, altbootcmd=run blf). NOT YET TESTED.
post_setup_verification.nor_hostname_command: echo ar300-nor-recovery > /proc/sys/kernel/hostname && echo ar300-nor-recovery > /etc/hostname && uci set system.@system[0].hostname='ar300-nor-recovery' && uci commit system
post_setup_verification.return_to_nand: From NOR: fw_setenv bootcount 0 && reboot. After reboot, NAND boots normally (bootcount resets on NAND boot).

recovery-http.notes: Flashed NAND factory.img (8.6MB) via U-Boot HTTP POST. ~4s upload, ~60s write+reboot. Switch LEFT.

nand_boot.notes: AR300M boots NAND OpenWrt 24.10.2 (hostname: switchleft, glinet,gl-ar300m-nand DTB). UBI rootfs with ~113MB overlay. Survived NOR setup (U-Boot upgrade + NOR firmware flash + bootargs fix). Also tested returning from NOR boot: switch LEFT + power cycle → NAND boots correctly, bootcount auto-resets to 0.

nor_setup.notes: setup-nor-recovery --i-want-a-brick completed all steps: U-Boot upgraded (2022-02-16), NOR firmware flashed (7MB to mtd2), boot_dev=on, bootargs fixed, boot_local=nor set. NAND boot verified surviving the setup.

nor_boot_switch_method.notes: FAILED. Switch RIGHT + boot_dev=on → select_boot_dev() tries bootm 0x9f050000 but returns (fails). Falls through to NAND boot. boot_local=nor also tried and failed. NOR partition verified correct (MD5 match). Root cause unknown.

nor_boot_bootcount_method.notes: SUCCESS. fw_setenv bootcount 3, power cycle. U-Boot: bootcount=4 > bootlimit=3 → nand_boot_failed=1 → bootm 0x9f050000 forced. NOR kernel boots: glinet,gl-ar300m-nor DTB, squashfs rootfs on NOR mtd4, jffs2 overlay 8.9MB. Hostname set to ar300-nor-recovery.

nand_boot_after_nor.notes: SUCCESS. After NOR boot (bootcount was 4), switched LEFT, power cycled. NAND booted correctly: hostname switchleft, glinet,gl-ar300m-nand DTB. Bootcount auto-reset to 0 on successful NAND boot. All U-Boot env changes persisted (bootargs fix, boot_dev=on, boot_local=nor). NAND boot survives complete NOR setup cycle.

usb-tether-android-adb.notes: Android phone via USB. Installed kmod-usb-net, kmod-usb-net-rndis, kmod-usb-net-cdc-ether, adb manually via SCP (no internet on router). ADB authorized (one-time phone-side accept). adb shell svc usb setFunctions rndis → usb0 appears (RNDIS). DHCP lease from phone. Ping 8.8.8.8 succeeds (38-178ms). Required packages: kmod-mii, kmod-usb-net, kmod-usb-net-cdc-ether, kmod-usb-net-rndis, adb, zlib, libopenssl3, libatomic1.

sysupgrade.description: AR300M uses glinet_nand_nor_do_upgrade() which auto-detects image type and flashes the correct partition regardless of current boot source.
sysupgrade.mechanism: Auto-detects by magic number: 0x27051956 (U-Boot Image Magic) → NOR upgrade (writes to NOR firmware partition), tar archive → NAND upgrade (writes to NAND kernel + UBI partitions). Source: openwrt/openwrt target/linux/ath79/nand/base-files/lib/upgrade/platform.sh, commit 55e6c903ae.
sysupgrade.nand_firmware_format: POSIX tar archive (sysupgrade.bin is actually tar containing kernel + rootfs + CONTROL metadata). Contains NAND kernel + UBI rootfs.
sysupgrade.nor_firmware_format: Raw uImage (U-Boot Image Magic 0x27051956, LZMA compressed MIPS kernel). Contains combined kernel+rootfs for NOR squashfs.
sysupgrade.after_nand_upgrade: Auto-sets fw_setenv bootcount 0 (next boot → NAND). Ensures device boots from newly flashed NAND.
sysupgrade.after_nor_upgrade: Auto-sets fw_setenv bootcount 3 (next boot → NOR via bootlimit). Ensures device boots from newly flashed NOR.
sysupgrade.notes: Both NAND and NOR images declare each other in SUPPORTED_DEVICES, enabling cross-flashing without --force. You can flash NAND firmware while booted from NOR, and vice versa. U-Boot env vars (bootcount, boot_dev, bootargs, boot_local) are preserved across sysupgrade (RAMFS_COPY_BIN includes fw_printenv/fw_setenv).
